## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification. Collapses depot identity in RBRR to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names, displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than hidden behind a timestamp.
  
  Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - RBRR field collapse: drop RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM, replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
  - Reshape rbgp_depot_levy: drop CLI positional arg, drop timestamp; project_id and sub-resource names come from RBDC kindle; trim post-levy banner to confirmation only.
  - Reshape rbgp_depot_unmake: drop CLI positional arg; target derives from RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER through RBDC.
  - Remove differential-furnish skip in rbgp_cli.sh so levy goes through zrbrr_enforce like every other depot operation.
  - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices beyond intent.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call at implementation.

  ## Character
  
  Foundational simplification. Collapses depot identity in RBRR to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names, displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than hidden behind a timestamp.
  
  Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - RBRR field collapse: drop RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM, replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
  - Reshape rbgp_depot_levy: drop CLI positional arg, drop timestamp; project_id and sub-resource names come from RBDC kindle; trim post-levy banner to confirmation only.
  - Reshape rbgp_depot_unmake: drop CLI positional arg; target derives from RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER through RBDC.
  - Remove differential-furnish skip in rbgp_cli.sh so levy goes through zrbrr_enforce like every other depot operation.
  - Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices beyond intent.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation.
  - 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call at implementation.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAM

## Character

Foundational simplification. Collapses depot identity in RBRR to (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) — project_id, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out of those two at kindle. Drops the timestamp from project_id; CLOUD_PREFIX flows uniformly through derived names, displacing today's hard-coded `rbw-`. Levy fails if derived resources already exist; the soft-delete window becomes operator-visible rather than hidden behind a timestamp.

Mechanical at call sites, conceptually load-bearing. Docket holds intent only — implementation specifics surface at commit time.

## Scope

- RBRR field collapse: drop RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY and RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM, replaced by RBDC-derived constants computed at kindle.
- Reshape rbgp_depot_levy: drop CLI positional arg, drop timestamp; project_id and sub-resource names come from RBDC kindle; trim post-levy banner to confirmation only.
- Reshape rbgp_depot_unmake: drop CLI positional arg; target derives from RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER through RBDC.
- Remove differential-furnish skip in rbgp_cli.sh so levy goes through zrbrr_enforce like every other depot operation.
- Marshal-zero blanking adjusted to match the new RBRR field set.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices beyond intent.

## Out of scope

- Bucket / Mason SA naming uniformity — design call at implementation.
- 409-fatal posture for currently-idempotent sub-resources — design call at implementation.

rbgg_list_service_accounts() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all service accounts in the project"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Listing service accounts in project: '"${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token (rc=$?)"

  buc_log_args 'List service accounts via REST API'
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts"                     "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}"

  local z_count
  z_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}" '.accounts | length') \
    || buc_die "Failed to parse response"

  if test "${z_count}" = "0"; then
    buc_info "No service accounts found in project"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_step "Found ${z_count} service account(s):"

  local z_max_width
  z_max_width=$(jq -r '.accounts[].email | length' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" | sort -n | tail -1) \
    || buc_die "Failed to calculate max width"

  jq -r --argjson width "${z_max_width}" \
    '.accounts[] | "  " + (.email | tostring | ((" " * ($width - length)) + .)) + " - " + (.displayName // "(no display name)")' \
    "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" || buc_die "Failed to format accounts"

  buc_success "Service account listing completed"
}

rbgg_charter_retriever() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_instance="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Charter Retriever service account instance"
  buc_doc_param "instance" "Instance name (required)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_instance}" || buc_die "Instance name required"
  zburd_sentinel
  test -d "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" || buc_die "BURD_OUTPUT_DIR does not exist: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_role_retriever}-${z_instance}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Chartering Retriever service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                                          \
    "${z_account_name}"                                                        \
    "Recipe Bottle Retriever (${z_instance})"                                  \
    "Read-only access to Google Artifact Registry - instance: ${z_instance}"   \
    "${z_instance}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Retriever SA"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Artifact Registry Reader role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Artifact Registry Reader"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "retriever-reader"

  buc_step 'Adding Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                              \
    "${z_token}"                                         \
    "Grant Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"                           \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"                  \
    "retriever-analysis"

  local z_actual_rbra_file="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_instance}.rbra"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_actual_rbra_file}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Install the RBRA file:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${z_actual_rbra_file} ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Or, for assay (test) mode:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${z_actual_rbra_file} ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
}

rbgg_knight_director() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_instance="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Knight Director service account instance"
  buc_doc_param "instance" "Instance name (required)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_instance}"     || buc_die "Instance name required"
  zburd_sentinel
  test -d "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" || buc_die "BURD_OUTPUT_DIR does not exist: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_role_director}-${z_instance}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Knighting Director service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                      \
    "${z_account_name}"                                    \
    "Recipe Bottle Director (${z_instance})"               \
    "Create/destroy container images for ${z_instance}"    \
    "${z_instance}" "${RBCC_role_director}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Director SA"

  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Cloud Build Editor role (project scope)'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Cloud Build Editor"              \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR}" \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-cb"

  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Project Viewer"                  \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "roles/viewer"                          \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-viewer"

  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Worker Pool User"               \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser"       \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-pool"

  buc_step 'Grant serviceAccountUser on Mason'
  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "${z_account_email}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser"

  buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  local z_gar_prop_delay=3
  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=420
  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
  local z_gar_get_infix=""
  local z_gar_set_infix=""
  local z_gar_get_code=""
  local z_gar_get_err=""

  while :; do
    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"

    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
    z_gar_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""

    # Propagation retry: newly-created Director SA may not be visible yet
    if test "${z_gar_get_code}" = "400"; then
      z_gar_get_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_err=""
      case "${z_gar_get_err}" in
        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
          buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
          continue
          ;;
      esac
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"

    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
    local z_gar_partial
    z_gar_partial=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgu_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_complete
    z_gar_complete=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

    local z_gar_set_code
    z_gar_set_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"

    # Propagation retry on SET
    if test "${z_gar_set_code}" = "400"; then
      local z_gar_set_err=""
      z_gar_set_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || z_gar_set_err=""
      case "${z_gar_set_err}" in
        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
          buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
          continue
          ;;
      esac
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
    break
  done

  local z_actual_rbra_file="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_instance}.rbra"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_actual_rbra_file}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Install the RBRA file:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${z_actual_rbra_file} ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Or, for assay (test) mode:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${z_actual_rbra_file} ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
}

rbgg_forfeit_service_account() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_sa_email="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Forfeit a service account"
  buc_doc_param "email" "Email address of the service account to forfeit"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

- `tt/rbw-aL.*` lists all three SAs with correct roles
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/charter/knight steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)

### phase-3-reliquary-and-hallmark-lifecycle (₢BBAAE) [rough]

## Character

Third executable phase. The largest cloud-side phase by surface area: exercises every producer mode (conjure, bind, graft) plus reliquary inscribe and full consumer flow (vouch, summon, plumb, tally). This is the phase where the recent first-build spook bug class (kludge-aware-charge-prereq, ZRBOB_PROJECT, reliquary integrity) gets caught at construction rather than discovery.

## Work

Add Phase 3 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Inscribe reliquary** — `rbw-dI` DirectorInscribesReliquary; verify reliquary stamp lands under `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}reliquaries/<date>/<tool>` with all canonical tools present (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo)
2. **Conjure small vessel** — `rbw-fO` against a small conjure-mode vessel (suggest `rbev-busybox` or sentry-deb-tether); verify hallmark publication with shape `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/{image,vouch,pouch,about,attest,diags}`
3. **Bind small vessel** — `rbw-fO` against a bind-mode vessel; verify digest pin and hallmark
4. **Graft small vessel** — `rbw-fk` LocalKludge then `rbw-fO` graft path; verify GRAFTED vouch verdict and `<hallmark>/` shape (no pouch for graft)
5. **Vouch all three** — `rbw-fV` mode-aware vouch; verify each verdict shape matches mode contract (SLSA for conjure, digest-pin for bind, GRAFTED for graft)
6. **Summon each** — `rbw-fs` RetrieverSummonsHallmark; verify local image present
7. **Plumb compact + full** — `rbw-fpc` and `rbw-fpf`; verify metadata fetch from new GAR layout
8. **Tally** — `rbw-ft` RetrieverTalliesHallmarks; verify all three hallmarks classified correctly by health state
9. **Rekon** — `rbw-irh` and `rbw-irr`; verify ark basenames match canonical members under hallmark and reliquary subtrees

Implicitly exercises the kludge-aware-charge-prereq fix path: k-prefixed kludge hallmarks classify correctly without false missing-vouch warnings.

## Verification

- All three hallmarks present in GAR with correct shape per mode
- Each vouch verdict matches mode contract
- Summon + plumb succeed against new layout
- Tally counts and classifications correct
- Rekon surfaces all expected canonical members
- No false missing-vouch warnings on k-prefixed paths

## Out of scope

- Crucible charge / security tests (Phase 4)
- Hallmark abjure (Phase 5)
- New vessel creation (uses existing kit-shipped vessels)

### phase-4-crucible-suite (₢BBAAF) [rough]

## Character

Fourth executable phase. Brings up containers; runs the security and capability fixtures against freshly-built hallmarks. This is where moriah's airgap test cases finally run on a depot whose first-time creation we just validated end-to-end. The user noted: "I've been pretending like I don't need to test depot creation but maybe I do" — this phase is where that decision becomes operational.

## Work

Add Phase 4 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Charge tadmor** — `rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh`; verify sentry+pentacle+bottle up cleanly under prefixed names
2. **Run tadmor security suite** — `rbtd-r.Run.tadmor.sh` (or single-case mode against the just-charged crucible); verify 34 security cases pass against a freshly-conjured tadmor hallmark
3. **Quench tadmor** — `rbw-cQ.Quench.tadmor.sh`; verify cleanup
4. **Charge moriah** — airgap nameplate; verify charge with airgap topology against fresh enshrined-base + airgap-conjured vessel
5. **Run moriah airgap suite** — capability fixtures verify airgap discipline holds end-to-end against fresh-cloud-built hallmark
6. **Quench moriah**
7. **Charge srjcl + run srjcl-jupyter (3 cases) + quench**
8. **Charge pluml + run pluml-diagram (5 cases) + quench**

Sequential only — fixtures share regime state and container/network namespaces. Per-fixture quench-after-test discipline so a failure in one fixture doesn't poison the next.

The moriah test specifically validates the airgap path that requires `rbw-dE` enshrine + airgap-mode `rbw-fO` to have completed in Phase 3 against the freshly-created depot. If Phase 3 was wrong, moriah fails here.

## Verification

- All four crucibles charge successfully against fresh hallmarks
- Tadmor security suite green
- Moriah airgap suite green (the test that motivated this whole heat)
- srjcl-jupyter green (3 cases)
- pluml-diagram green (5 cases)
- Charge/quench cycles leave no residual state between fixtures

## Out of scope

- Cleanup of hallmarks/SAs/depot (Phase 5)
- Test infrastructure changes (any test failures here surface in subsequent paces or follow-up heats)
- Adding new fixtures (this phase exercises the existing four)

### phase-5-cleanup-and-first-end-to-end-run (₢BBAAG) [rough]

## Character

Cleanup phase plus the actual first run. The tier doesn't ship until it's been run end-to-end against fresh state and surprises captured. Failures during the first run are *informative*, not blockers — they document the surprises this whole heat exists to surface.

## Work

### Cleanup phase implementation

Add Phase 5 to `rbw-tP`:

1. **Abjure all hallmarks** — `rbw-fA` for each ordained hallmark; verify clean removal via `rbw-irh`
2. **Forfeit service accounts** — `rbw-aF` for retriever, director, governor (in dependency order, governor last since it grants the others); verify clean removal via `rbw-aL`
3. **Unmake depot** — `rbw-dU` PayorUnmakesDepot; verify quota recovery
4. **Final tally** — `rbw-dl` shows no depots, `rbw-irh`/`rbw-irr` surfaces clean GAR namespace

### First end-to-end run

Execute the FIRST end-to-end run of `rbw-tP` against actual fresh state:

1. Marshal zero, commit (using the new signature from Phase 0)
2. Install RBRAs from secure backup
3. Run `rbw-tP` — full sequence; expect ~1 hour wall-clock
4. Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX — in a notch dedicated to this pace's findings
5. Fix small surprises inline within this pace (one-line bash fixes, message clarity)
6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run

### phase-6-prep-release-integration (₢BBAAH) [rough]

## Character

Final integration pace. Wires pristine-pass as a precondition for `/rbk-prep-release` so the upstream contribution ceremony cannot proceed without a recent pristine green. Closes the loop: pristine qualification becomes load-bearing on release prep, not optional ceremony.

## Work

Modify `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` to add a precondition step at or near step 1:

- Verify a recent `rbw-tP` run was green
- Recency contract — design call: most recent commit on main? Last 7 days? Tagged release marker on the green run? Suggest "pristine-passed marker file references HEAD's commit SHA exactly" — strongest invariant, prevents stale passes
- If pristine has not run green against the current HEAD, prep-release fails-fast with operator-visible message naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence

Likely needs a small artifact written by `rbw-tP` on success — e.g., a `.pristine-passed` file (gitignored) carrying timestamp + commit SHA. `/rbk-prep-release` reads it; if SHA doesn't match HEAD, prep-release refuses.

Update README "Release Procedure" section (from Pace 1) to reflect that step 4 (`/rbk-prep-release`) now enforces step 3 (`rbw-tP`) had to pass against the very HEAD that prep-release is preparing.

## Verification

- `/rbk-prep-release` fails fast when no pristine pass is recorded for current HEAD
- After successful `rbw-tP`, `/rbk-prep-release` proceeds normally
- After successful `rbw-tP` followed by an unrelated commit, `/rbk-prep-release` refuses (SHA mismatch)
- Pristine-passed artifact's gitignore status is correct (deliberately not committed)
- README accurately documents the precondition

## Out of scope

- Restructuring the rest of `/rbk-prep-release` (preserved as-is)
- Multi-pristine-run aggregation
- CI integration
- Recency policies beyond exact-HEAD-match (other policies left for future heats if needed)

content.text:
You were right — most of my open questions ARE answered across sibling paces. The malformation is that BBAAN's docket lists a thin "settled" set, but cross-pace siblings settle more.

## Cross-pace settlements I missed

**From ₢BBAAQ (multi-fact-cult-verb-spec):**
- "RBS0 cult-verb concepts" → producer-output discipline: **assay slot as binding output for credential bytes; retirement of BURD-output-dir as canonical credential write**.
- Per-row facts via type-as-extension naming.
- BUF_EXT_* constant convention (closed registry, value-matches-name-downcased) is the BUS0 pattern BBAAN should follow.

**From ₢BBAAR (handbook-cult-verb-sweep):**
- BBAAN must produce **new RBYC_* and RBZ_* constants** (replacing `RBYC_CHARTER`/`RBYC_KNIGHT`, `RBZ_CHARTER_RETRIEVER`/`RBZ_KNIGHT_DIRECTOR`/`RBZ_LIST_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS`/etc.) — handbook depends on these.
- Verb-as-noun **ceremony cadence**: `(Charter):` → `(Invest):` (capitalized, parenthetical preserved).
- `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` confirmed unchanged.

**From ₢BBAAK (phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed):**
- Per-role split confirmed: "**retriever invest, director invest**" and "**divests of operational SAs**" (plural) — two musters and two divests, not unified.
- Test-side primitive: "**file copy (assay → role production path)**" — this is the canonical flow the producer-output discipline implies. Confirms singleton-per-role production paths and assay-only producer write.
- Manifest delta: "add new cult-verb colophons; **remove the colophons retired by BBAAN**".

## Open-questions revisited

| # | Question | Status after grooming |
|---|----------|----------------------|
| 1 | Colophon letter scheme | **Open** — paddock fixes shape (`rbw-a<role><verb>`), not single-letter vs full-word. Existing aC/aK/aL/aF/aM precedent leans single-letter. |
| 2 | Function naming | **Open** — mount time. |
| 3 | Singleton invest args | **Settled** — assay-only producer output + BBAAK's file-copy flow imply zero-instance-arg. |
| 4 | Per-role vs unified muster | **Settled** — per-role per BBAAQ+BBAAK plurality. |
| 5 | Fact extension shape | **Mostly settled** — type-as-extension; per-role likely (matching BBAAK's "type-extension lookup"). Exact constant names at mount. |
| 6 | Fact content | **Mostly settled** — presence-as-the-fact precedent (BBAAO's BUF_EXT_ALIAS); empty content unless mount surfaces a need. |
| 7 | Divest post-delete cleanup | **Open** — no sibling speaks to it. |

So **the genuinely open** decisions at mount time are: (1) colophon letter scheme, (2) function naming, (5) exact fact-ext constant names, (7) post-divest RBRA file disposition. Plus instance-arg-removal mechanics in (3).

## Proposed remedy

Reslate BBAAN's docket to absorb the cross-pace settlements. Specifically:
- Expand the "Settled-at-paddock decisions" Constraint with: per-role muster split, per-role divest split, singleton invest (zero-arg, assay-exclusive write), RBYC/RBZ constant emission as a build product of this pace, and manifest delta affordance for BBAAK.
- Move (4), (5), and (6) from the implicit-open pile into the settled pile, with the residue-open list named explicitly.
- Add a "Cross-pace dependencies" subsection naming BBAAQ, BBAAR, BBAAK as consumers of this pace's settled vocabulary.

Want me to draft the reslated docket for your review?

content.text:
No — BBAAN is the only owner.

**Current tabtarget state** (verified): the original five (aC, aF, aK, aL, aM) are still there. Nothing created, nothing eliminated.

**Search across all heats:** only BBAAN, BBAAQ, BBAAR, BBAAK touch this surface. Of those:
- **BBAAQ** is spec — describes verbs, doesn't define colophons.
- **BBAAR** is handbook prose — adapts narrative to whatever BBAAN settles ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles").
- **BBAAK** explicitly says "**Manifest registration: add new cult-verb colophons; remove the colophons retired by BBAAN**" — i.e., it consumes BBAAN's settlements, doesn't define them.

So BBAAN gets to define the exact colophon strings. Its docket constraint ("No pre-staged ... exact colophon strings") just means we hold them open until mount-time discussion. No prior pace authority overrides this.

**Useful corroboration the search surfaced:** `muster` is already a working verb in the codebase — `rbz_zipper.sh:127-129` enrolls `RBZ_MUSTER_HALLMARKS` / `RBZ_MUSTER_RELIQUARIES` / `RBZ_MUSTER_ENSHRINEMENTS` for `rbw-imh` / `rbw-imr` / `rbw-ime` with functions `rbfl_muster_hallmarks` etc. That gives BBAAN a strong precedent for both:
- Constant naming: `RBZ_MUSTER_<NOUN>` → `RBZ_MUSTER_RETRIEVERS`, `RBZ_MUSTER_DIRECTORS`
- Function naming: `<prefix>_muster_<plural-noun>` → `rbgg_muster_retrievers`, `rbgg_muster_directors`
- Colophon shape: `rbw-im{h,r,e}` is **two-letter-then-classifier** (`im` = image-muster, third letter = noun). For BBAAN: `rbw-a<role><verb>` could analogously be `rbw-a{r,d}{i,d,m}` — three-letter colophon: `a` (account family), then role, then verb.

So the whole verb triad has live precedent for muster, and the rest can follow the same shape pattern.

Ready to discuss colophon letter choices and reslate the docket whenever you want.

input.content:
# jjezs_reslate BBAAN

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; muster replaces list. Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Muster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, muster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Muster | `rbw-arm` | `rbw-adm` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (m) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Muster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (current pattern continues, but driver is regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for muster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Muster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged function names, exact constant strings beyond those in Settled, or step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; muster replaces list. Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Muster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, muster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Muster | `rbw-arm` | `rbw-adm` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (m) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Muster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (current pattern continues, but driver is regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for muster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Muster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged function names, exact constant strings beyond those in Settled, or step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).

content.text:
Reslate landed cleanly.

## Remaining open questions for mount

These survive the reslate — genuinely mount-time decisions:

**1. Function naming pattern.** Two readable shapes:
- `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` — `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_muster_retrievers`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` / `rbgg_knight_director` precedent.
- `rbgg_<role>_<verb>` — groups by role first.

The image-muster precedent (`rbfl_muster_hallmarks`, `rbfl_muster_reliquaries`, `rbfl_muster_enshrinements`) is `<verb>_<noun>`, supporting the first shape.

**2. Fact-extension constant.** Two viable shapes:
- Per-role: `RBGG_FACT_EXT_RETRIEVER="retriever"`, `RBGG_FACT_EXT_DIRECTOR="director"`. Basename = SA email or moniker. Cleaner type-extension lookup.
- Single: `RBGG_FACT_EXT_SA="sa"`. Basename carries role (e.g., `retriever-foo.sa`).

Per-role aligns with the per-role colophon split philosophy and matches BBAAK's "type-extension lookup" phrasing.

**3. Fact content shape.** Three options:
- Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches BUF_EXT_ALIAS precedent).
- Carries SA email.
- Carries SA email + status (active / pending-deletion / etc.).

Empty is the safe default; muster currently has no status to convey beyond presence, but the email could spare consumers a separate API call.

**4. Divest post-delete RBRA disposition.** Two options:
- Delete the role's production RBRA file after successful cloud DELETE (mirrors cloud state; operator can't accidentally use revoked credentials).
- Leave the file (operator-driven cleanup; conservative).

Tilts toward delete — divest's purpose is removing the role; leaving stale credentials defeats the intent.

**5. Invest idempotency.** Behavior when SA already exists in cloud:
- Fatal (clean-state assumption; matches the levy "fails if exists" posture from BBAAM).
- Idempotent (re-fetch existing SA, regenerate key, write RBRA).

The pristine-tier philosophy in BB's paddock favors fatal. But operators outside pristine flow may want to re-key a role without divest-then-invest.

**6. Defensive depot-unmake when governor absent.** Does unmake need to handle the case where mantle hasn't run? Operationally rare; could fail-fast with a clear message or skip silently.

Want to walk through these in order, or jump to one?

content.text:
Got 1–5. Captured.

**On #6 — clarification:** the BBAAN docket says "Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb." So when `rbgp_depot_unmake` runs, somewhere in its sequence it now needs to delete the governor SA too. The question is what happens if an operator runs unmake before ever running mantle — i.e., the governor SA doesn't exist in the depot.

**Simplest: tolerate 404.** The existing `rbgg_forfeit_service_account` already does this:

```bash
rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}" \
  404 "not found (already deleted)"
```

When unmake folds in governor cleanup, just reuse the same 404-tolerant pattern. No special detection, no ceremonial diagnostic, no fail-fast logic. The DELETE call returns 200 if it existed, 404 if it didn't, both fine — the depot teardown proceeds regardless. Matches existing semantics; zero new code complexity. That's the resolution.

---

Decisions to persist into the docket (captured from your answers):

1. Function naming: `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` — six functions.
2. Fact constants: live in RBCC as tinder constants, one contiguous block. Includes migrating existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into the same block. (Will confirm exact constant naming at mount based on RBCC's existing convention.)
3. Fact content: empty.
4. Divest: delete role's production RBRA file after successful cloud DELETE.
5. Invest: fatal if SA already exists in cloud.
6. Unmake-governor-absent: 404-tolerant via existing `rbgu_http_require_ok` 404-OK pattern. No special handling.

Want me to do a second reslate absorbing these into a new "Implementation conventions" section, or hold them in conversation for the actual mount? My read: persist them — they're durable decisions and we shouldn't risk re-deriving them from scratch later.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; muster replaces list. Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Muster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, muster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Muster | `rbw-arm` | `rbw-adm` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (m) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Muster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_muster_retrievers`, `rbgg_muster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent and the `rbfl_muster_*` image-muster precedent.

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Muster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

"..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).

Five handbook files in `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`, all with paddock-settled vocabulary by the time this pace mounts.

**rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh — payor wrapper.** `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` reference (L95) — confirm mantle stays unchanged per BBAAN's cult-verb decision (mantle stays as singleton-per-depot exception). Producer-output narrative for governor RBRA placement parallels rbhogw.

**rbhob_base.sh — shared helpers.** `zrbho_credential_install` may need adjustment if BBAAN settles assay-slot-exclusive write semantics — base helper changes ripple consistently across all credential install narratives.

These behaviors must surface in narrative; the handbook is the operator's contract.

- Handbook narrative describes what landed in BBAAN — read the actual code state, do not work from intent. If implementation differs from paddock-settled vocabulary, raise it; do not silently reconcile.

- Use existing `buh_line` / `buh_step` / `buh_section` / `buh_tt` primitives — no narrative-structure rework.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices.

- Handbook files outside the five listed — depot-identity narrative is already swept by BBAAM Step 1; non-SA handbooks (crucible, windows, tadmor, first-crucible, dispatch CLI) don't touch the changing surfaces.
- Spec/README/RBYC vocabulary documentation — owned by sibling pace ₢BBAAQ.
- Restructuring handbook section ordering or step decomposition — out of scope for a vocabulary sweep.
- New onboarding content — out of scope.

- Reuse paddock-locked patterns; do not re-engineer them here.

## Out of scope

- SA-management surface refactor (BBAAN).
- Phase 1+ of rbw-tP (BBAAC and onward).
- Multi-instance test scenarios beyond single-instance per role.
- Case 1 (marshal-zero gate) — stays in §1, untouched by this rebuild.
